summary: Partially decompiled the fighter color-animation module by adding `src/melee/ft/ftcolanim.c`, wiring it into the build, and shrinking the old assembly file from a full text implementation to a small data/literal residue. The C translation introduced function-pointer dispatch tables, simple wrapper callbacks into `ftAction_*`, lb color-animation helper calls, inline-ASM fallbacks for still-hard functions, and supporting Fighter struct/header declarations.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/ftcolanim.c` with 650 lines of C/inline-ASM content for the `ftcolanim` module.

Removed most of `asm/melee/ft/ftcolanim.s` text implementation, deleting 607 lines and leaving only a small amount of assembly/data residue including exported literal/data labels such as `ftCo_803C6AE8`, `ftCo_803C6B08`, and `ftCo_804D3C10`.
Added `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ftcolanim.c.o` to `obj_files.mk` so the new C file participates in the build.

Introduced `FtCmd` dispatch tables `ftCo_803C6AD0` and `ftCo_803C6ADC`, mapping command cases to small static wrappers around `ftAction_80071028`, `ftAction_800711DC`, `ftAction_80071B50`, `ftAction_80071CA4`, `ftAction_800730B8`, and `ftAction_80073108`.

Converted trivial public functions such as `ftCo_Sleep_Anim`, `ftCo_Sleep_IASA`, `ftCo_800BFFAC`, and `ftCo_800C0074` into C bodies, with the latter two forwarding to `lb_80014498` on Fighter subfields.

Kept harder routines as `ASM` functions guarded by `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` with `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stubs outside matching mode, preserving exact assembly under `MUST_MATCH` for functions including `ft_800BFF34`, `ftCo_800BFFD0`, `ft_800C0098`, `ftCo_800C0134`, `ftCo_800C0200`, `ftCo_800C0358`, and `ftCo_800C0408`.

Expanded `struct Fighter` in `src/melee/ft/types.h` by replacing broad filler around offsets `0x3F8` through `0x584` with individual `UNK_T xNNN` fields so code can address color-animation-related subregions such as `x408`, `x488`, and `x508` directly.

Adjusted `ftcolanim.h` prototypes, removing exported declarations for static/internal dispatch helpers and adding more specific parameter names/types where known, including `ftCo_800C0408(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.
smart_moves: Split obviously simple callback wrappers into real C while isolating the harder control-flow-heavy routines behind `ASM`/`MUST_MATCH` blocks, allowing incremental progress without blocking the whole file on difficult matches.
Preserved dispatch-table structure explicitly with `FtCmd ftCo_803C6AD0[3]` and `ftCo_803C6ADC[3]`, matching the original indirect `mtlr`/`blrl` behavior instead of flattening the logic prematurely.
Used exact Fighter offset fields (`x408`, `x488`, `x508`) rather than pointer arithmetic on filler arrays, making later cleanup and semantic naming easier.
Moved build ownership from the `.s` text body to the new `.c.o` while leaving residual literals/data in asm, a practical hybrid step for partial decompilation.
Added lb and ftdata prototypes needed by the new C file instead of relying entirely on `UNK_PARAMS` call sites.
decomp_lessons: For partial file matches, it is acceptable to land a hybrid C file with exact inline-ASM fallbacks when it removes a large assembly blob and exposes structure/prototype information for future work.

Function-pointer tables are often the cleanest C representation for PowerPC dispatch sequences that load a function pointer, move it to LR, and `blrl`; preserve the table order and index arithmetic exactly.
When code repeatedly addresses fixed object offsets, expanding struct filler into individual `UNK_T` fields can unlock C expressions like `&fp->x488` while still avoiding premature semantic guesses.
Keep hard-matching functions behind `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` plus exact assembly to preserve build modes during incremental decompilation.
Adding header declarations for newly called helper functions is part of decomp progress; avoid hiding calls behind unknown signatures when a minimal prototype is known.
Evidence for semantic names in this PR is limited; most new Fighter fields remain offset-based `xNNN` names, which is appropriate until their purpose is proven.
naming_conventions: Common fighter functions use the `ftCo_` prefix for common fighter code and retain address-based suffixes such as `ftCo_800BFFD0` when no semantic name is known.
Static local wrappers are named by address, e.g. `ftCo_800BFE74`, and declared `static` when only used by local dispatch tables.
Global data/literal labels use address-style names such as `ftCo_803C6AD0`, `ftCo_803C6ADC`, `ftCo_803C6AE8`, `ftCo_803C6B08`, and `ftCo_804D3C10`.
Unknown Fighter fields are represented as offset names like `x408`, `x488`, and `x508` rather than invented semantic names.
Header prototypes were refined with parameter names like `Fighter* fp` and `Fighter_GObj* gobj` where the role was evident.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `ASM` functions with `nofralloc` exact assembly blocks for routines not yet converted to matching C.
Guarded fallback source with `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` and `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`, while keeping exact assembly in the `#else` matching path.
Included `#pragma peephole on` after inline assembly blocks, preserving compiler behavior around hand-written assembly.
Represented command dispatch in C with arrays of `FtCmd` and `ftCo_803C6ADC[arg0 - 0x15](gobj, cmd)` for at least one non-MUST/WIP body.
Used `#pragma force_active on` under `WIP`, likely to retain otherwise-unused data/literals during work-in-progress builds.
Kept literal/data labels in `asm/melee/ft/ftcolanim.s` while moving text routines into `src/melee/ft/ftcolanim.c`.
follow_up_queries: Can the inline-ASM routines in `src/melee/ft/ftcolanim.c` be replaced with matching C now that the dispatch tables and Fighter offset fields are exposed?
What are the semantic meanings of Fighter fields `x408`, `x488`, and `x508` used by color-animation helper calls?
Can `lb_80014498`, `lb_800144C8`, and `lb_80014258` receive stronger types now that `ftcolanim` call sites identify likely argument shapes?
Can the residual literals/data in `asm/melee/ft/ftcolanim.s` be moved into C once string/data placement is understood?
Can `ftData_UnkMotionStates4` be renamed after more call sites establish its role?
